resources.name: Search The Watch Market — /data Business API
resources.description: Business API for watch market data. Requires a Business API subscription.

resources.name: Health
resources.description: Service health check — no authentication required

resources.name: Model Endpoints
resources.description: Endpoints scoped to a watch model group (group_id)

resources.name: Resolve
resources.description: Resolve a free-text brand + model string to a group_id

resources.name: Listing Endpoints
resources.description: Endpoints scoped to an individual listing (listing_id)

resources.name: Health Check
resources.description: Verify the service is running. No Authorization header needed.

resources.name: Current Prices
resources.description:
Returns the latest complete weekly price snapshot for a model group: min, max, mean and market value from the most recent fully completed historic week.

USD is always used when the weekly snapshot contains USD values. If USD is unavailable, the endpoint falls back to the first populated supported currency. listing_count is the relatedProductsCount for that week, not a live active-listing count.

Replace group_id in the environment (default: 2314 = Omega Speedmaster Moonwatch 310.32.42.50.01.002).

resources.name: Model Specifications
resources.description:
Returns the consensus specification properties for a watch model group — brand, reference number, case diameter, movement caliber, water resistance, etc.

Data is served from the file cache where available, otherwise proxied from the main API.

resources.name: Price History (last 3 months)
resources.description:
Returns the last 13 complete weeks (~3 months) of weekly average price history for a model group. Each week entry includes mean, min and max prices expressed in all 11 supported currencies simultaneously (GBP, USD, EUR, AUD, CAD, CHF, HKD, SGD, PLN, AED, JPY).

This is the same dataset that powers the historic price chart on the website.

resources.name: New Listings — first page
resources.description:
Returns new listings added in the last 7 days for a watch model group. 20 items per page, ordered newest first with stable cursor pagination.

Omit the cursor on the first request. Use the next_cursor value from the response to fetch the next page. Listing IDs from this response can be passed to the individual listing history endpoint.

resources.name: New Listings — next cursor page
resources.description: Use this after the first request. Paste the previous response's next_cursor value into the cursor query parameter.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Paste next_cursor from the previous response

resources.name: Resolve brand + model to group_id
resources.description:
Turns a free-text brand + model string into a group_id.

Examples:
  ?q=rolex+submariner+126610ln
  ?q=rolex+16233
  ?q=ap+royal+oak+15500st
  ?q=panerai+pam01661
  ?q=jlc+reverso+250186

Normalisation is applied automatically: case-insensitive, diacritics stripped, brand aliases resolved (jlc, ap, pp, rm, …), Panerai PAM spacing collapsed, Rolex two-letter suffix merged.

Returns 404 if no catalogue match exists.

resources.name: Individual Listing Price History
resources.description:
Returns the full price change history for a single listing — every distinct asking price recorded and the date it was first observed.

Each entry is a deduplicated price point; only genuine price changes produce new entries. The array is ordered oldest first, making it easy to plot price-over-time.

Works for both active and inactive (sold/removed) listings.
